title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I heard that you can get a meal at the cafeteria for chonon. So I'm going to head there now and grab a bite to eat.

title: will / shall
examples: I think we should go that way. Then we will pass like college of art and then college of shimany. Let's go!
And in these years, I've noticed a lot of students will take a gap year just to take a breather or to think about what they want to do or pursue an internship.
The gap years aren't very common and people will start to do internships very early on.

A lot of people will just tap or scan, then they will leave really fast before the professor walks in.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: I will be continuing my studies for the next three years.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: And then I would say junior and senior year is really when you start to think about what you want to do.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I heard that you can get a meal at the cafeteria for chonon. So I'm going to head there now and grab a bite to eat.
Let's find somewhere to sit. Wow, I can't believe this is only chonon. That's less than one US dollar.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: Yeah, doing homework and studying all the time. It must be nice to walk outside and enjoy this nature.
It must be really tough here.

title: have to / must
examples: It's very peaceful. The students must need this. They have such a stressful life.
You have to work hard to get to classes.
But because you have to fulfill a certain number of credits.
They just had to make sure that you didn't have alcohol in your room.
So I feel like you must get a lot of homework.

title: should (advice)
examples: I think we should go that way. Then we will pass like college of art and then college of shimany. Let's go!

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: But then a difference, I would say, is that you have more time in college in Korea to think about what you want to do.
And then I would say junior and senior year is really when you start to think about what you want to do.
So he'd always give us a warning.
He'd knock on the door and say, if you have anything to hide, hide it now.
We have a system where you can just tap your ID card on this attendance checker.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: He'd knock on the door and say, if you have anything to hide, hide it now.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: Or maybe that's also because I'm a foreigner. I'm very lucky to have these kinders. I love how this campus is surrounded by nature.

title: have something done
examples: I heard that you can get a meal at the cafeteria for chonon. So I'm going to head there now and grab a bite to eat.
